description_long: This object is a slender, pointed knife with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a fine tip, suitable for piercing. The blade is metallic and has a dark, matte finish, giving it a utilitarian and somewhat tactical appearance. The handle is cylindrical with a subtle ergonomic contour, finished in a metallic bronze or brass tone, and appears to be made of metal or a metal-plated material. A small, flat crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing a functional barrier for hand protection. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The knife is compact and appears well-balanced for precise handling, making it suitable for utility or self-defense purposes in an urban environment.
description: A straight, double-edged knife with a dark metallic blade and a contoured bronze-toned handle, separated by a small crossguard. The design is minimalistic and functional, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, pointed blade and the contoured metallic handle.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow blade profile and the circular end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the blade and handle features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the handle and the thin blade edge.
attribute_handle_design: contoured metallic handle, cylindrical, smooth finish, no visible grip texture
